summary: A lightweight, single-threaded Python CLI tool that reads CSV files incrementally, validates structure and content, and converts to JSON with configurable output format. Designed for simplicity, portability, and robust error reporting without external dependencies.

components.name: CLI Entrypoint
components.responsibility: Parses command-line arguments, orchestrates workflow, handles help/usage, and manages exit codes

components.name: CSV Validator
components.responsibility: Validates CSV syntax (e.g., consistent column count, delimiter consistency), detects empty lines, checks for header presence, and reports structural errors

components.name: CSV Reader
components.responsibility: Streams CSV rows iteratively to minimize memory usage; supports optional header detection and field mapping
interfaces: read_rows(file_path: str, skip_header: bool = True) -> Iterator[dict | list]

components.name: JSON Converter
components.responsibility: Transforms validated CSV rows into JSON structures (list-of-objects or object-with-keyed-records), applies field mappings, and serializes output
interfaces: convert(rows: Iterator, format: str = 'array', key_field: str = None, field_map: dict = None) -> str

components.name: Error Handler
components.responsibility: Centralizes error logging, formatting, and recovery decisions (e.g., continue vs. abort on row-level errors)
interfaces: log_error(context: str, message: str, fatal: bool = False)

deployment_strategy: Distribute as standalone executable via PyInstaller or as source package installable via pip (e.g., `pip install .`). No server or runtime infrastructure required.
security_considerations: Input file paths are validated to prevent path traversal (e.g., reject '../' patterns)
No code execution or template injection — only parsing of structured data
File I/O is read-only unless explicit output path is provided; original CSV is never modified
No network calls or external resource access
scalability_notes: Uses streaming CSV reading to support files >1GB with constant memory footprint (~O(columns) per row)
No parallelism — intentional for simplicity and determinism; sufficient for typical CLI use cases
Extensible architecture allows future plugins (e.g., custom validators) without core changes
